name: Profile & Security Settings
description: View/update profile, change password, and set up/manage multi-factor authentication from within the authenticated dashboard.

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:1595 (Update Password button), 1602-1607 (MFA status badge + setup body), 2798-2823 (changePassword(), calling POST /api/auth/change-password), 2825+ (MFA setup flow) — all read directly, verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:897 sidebar nav-item (data-page="settings") reaches this page section.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/phase10PasswordReset.test.mjs, workers/test/mfaEnforcementRegression.test.mjs, and workers/test/gdprAccountDeletion.test.mjs import directly from workers/src/handlers/auth.js — confirmed by import-statement grep, 2026-07-08.

verification.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:1595-1607,2798-2825 read directly; workers/test/phase10PasswordReset.test.mjs and workers/test/mfaEnforcementRegression.test.mjs import statements confirmed.

notes: No actionable gap found in this pass. P7 assigned by elimination (no P1-P6 condition applies).

name: Scan History & Reports
description: View past scans/purchased reports with expiry and re-download.

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:3700-3722 (loadUserReports()) renders the reports table with module/target/price/expiry/download columns and is wired to fire on nav to the reports page or on load if already active. As of commit 67f6b924 (PR #97 quick-win fix), this function is the sole owner of #reports-table — the previous race condition against loadPayments() (both functions writing the same DOM element, whichever resolved last silently won) was removed by scoping loadPayments() to #payment-history only. Independently re-confirmed 2026-07-08: no other function in the file references #reports-table.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/billingPortal.test.mjs and workers/test/commercialPlatform.test.mjs reference report_url/customer payments behavior (confirmed by grep for these terms; both test the payments/billing data shape this page consumes).

verification.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:3700-3722 read directly and cross-checked against the PR #97 diff (git show 67f6b924) to confirm the previously-documented race condition is actually fixed in current code, not just claimed fixed.

notes: This entry supersedes docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:227's 'Exists, buggy' status — the bug it references (Quick Win #3) was fixed in commit 67f6b924, after the mission brief was written. No actionable gap found now; P7 by elimination.

name: Session Management (Active Sessions / Per-Session Revoke)
description: Let a customer see which devices/sessions are currently signed in and revoke an individual session, rather than only being able to sign out everywhere at once.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-10 (production-readiness lifecycle wave 1). New 'Active Sessions' card added to #page-settings between Change Password and 2FA, table body #sessions-list. loadSessions() (lazy-loaded the first time Settings is opened, same pattern as loadMFAStatus()) calls GET /api/user/sessions with an X-Session-Hint header carrying the browser's own already-held refresh token (sessionStorage 'cdb_refresh') purely so the backend can flag which row is 'This device' — not a new privilege, the token is already held client-side and already usable at /api/auth/refresh. renderSessions() shows a device label parsed from user_agent (sessDeviceLabel()), IP, signed-in date, and a per-row 'Sign out' button (hidden for the current session) wired via a data-session-id attribute + revokeSession(this.dataset.sessionId) — not a string-interpolated onclick, deliberately following the CAP-ADMIN-004 fix's safer pattern even though session ids are server-generated hex (no actual injection vector) rather than the older, riskier convention.

navigation.evidence: Embedded in the existing, already-discoverable Settings page (data-page="settings"), same sidebar nav-item as Change Password / 2FA.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/userSessionManagement.test.mjs (new, 2026-07-10, 13 tests, real in-memory node:sqlite matching production schema, same convention as workers/test/phase10PasswordReset.test.mjs): lists only the caller's own active non-revoked non-expired sessions, flags the current session via X-Session-Hint without leaking token_hash to the client, returns the device/IP/created_at fields the UI renders, requires authentication on both routes, revokes an owned session, 404s (not silently succeeds) on another user's session id (IDOR-safe) and on an already-revoked/nonexistent id, plus a route+frontend contract check (workers/src/index.js registers both routes wired to the real handlers; frontend/user-dashboard.html calls the real endpoints, not a placeholder; the revoke button uses a data-attribute, not a string-interpolated onclick).

verification.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-10. workers/src/handlers/auth.js (handleListSessions/handleRevokeSession), workers/src/index.js (route registration + auditLog on revoke), frontend/user-dashboard.html (#page-settings card, loadSessions/renderSessions/revokeSession/sessDeviceLabel/sessEsc) all read directly. All 3 inline <script> blocks in frontend/user-dashboard.html syntax-checked clean (new Function(src)) before and after the change. Full backend suite green: 191 files / 2038 tests (190/2025 baseline + 1 new file/13 tests, zero regressions).

notes: customer_journey_complete stays false pending a real dynamic_browser (live production click-through) pass — this fix used static evidence + a real in-memory-SQL-backed handler test, not a headless-Chromium session, per this registry's own Production Truth Law (docs/ENGINEERING_STANDARDS.md §11). Deliberately minimal scope, matching the capability's own description exactly: list + individual revoke only. NOT built in this pass (disclosed, not silently skipped): a 'sign out all other sessions' bulk action (would need a new backend function that excludes the caller's own current token, distinct from the existing revokeAllUserTokens which revokes literally everything including the caller); a 'last used' timestamp (refresh_tokens has no such column — created_at is the closest available proxy and is what's shown); geo/location display (would require an IP-geolocation dependency, out of scope for this fix). Matches docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md Domain 1 (Identity) and Domain 9 (Customer Portal) rows, which both flagged this same gap before the fix.

name: Support Ticket System
description: In-product support ticket submission and tracking, as opposed to routing every support request through email.

entry_points: POST /api/support/ticket, GET /api/support/tickets/mine, GET /api/support/ticket/:id, POST /api/support/ticket/:id/comment, POST /api/support/ticket/:id/status (plus pre-existing FAQ/status/SLA/docs/admin-list endpoints)

routes_sampled.evidence: workers/src/handlers/support.js:513-514 -- POST, not PATCH: workers/src/middleware/cors.js:49's Access-Control-Allow-Methods omits PATCH platform-wide (a pre-existing, cross-cutting gap affecting several other routes already, e.g. workers/src/index.js:5570 and :8309 -- out of scope to fix in this pass, flagged separately below).

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:974 (nav item), :2581 (page-support dedicated page: My Tickets list + ticket detail/comment thread view + Help Center tab), :2736 (Create Ticket modal), :5827 (loadSupport() and the full loadMyTickets/createTicket/openTicketDetail/addTicketComment/updateTicketStatus/loadHelpCenter function set). Reuses apiFetch()/showToast()/openModal()/closeModal() -- no new client-side helpers introduced.

navigation.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:974 -- a real sidebar nav-item ("🎫 Support") in the Account section, same pattern as Billing/Settings.

permissions: org_members: any active role (OWNER/ADMIN/ANALYST/MEMBER/VIEWER) may view/comment on their org's tickets
ticket_owner: user_id match on a personal (org-less) ticket
isAdmin: platform staff, full access incl. staff-only 'closed' status

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/supportTicketOrgScopeAndComments.test.mjs (25 tests: auth requirement, org scoping incl. cross-org 403/404 isolation, personal vs org-scoped ticket lists, comment authorization, staff-vs-customer notification behavior, status-transition RBAC). workers/test/copilotSalesSupportTools.test.mjs updated (ad-hoc CREATE TABLE gained organization_id/updated_at columns to match the new schema) and still green (7 tests, unchanged count).

verification.evidence: Customer Lifecycle Completion Program, Phase 3: workers/schema_v51_support_ticket_org_scope.sql adds support_tickets.organization_id (nullable, additive) + support_ticket_comments; workers/src/handlers/support.js extended with handleMyTickets/handleTicketDetail/handleAddComment/handleUpdateStatus, org-membership checks against org_members (same pattern as workers/src/handlers/aiMaturityHandler.js, PR #257), and in-app notifications via workers/src/handlers/notificationPlatform.js's deliverNotification(). Ticket creation (workers/src/handlers/support.js:222-225) now requires a real user id -- closes a prior unauthenticated-ticket-spam gap; the AI-copilot create_support_ticket tool (workers/src/handlers/aiSecurityCopilot.js:1841-1856) forwards its own session authCtx unchanged and continues to work. Verified dynamically: all 25 new tests plus the full worker suite (300 files / 3169 tests, up from the pre-change 299/3144) pass against a real node:sqlite-backed D1 double driving the actual handleSupport() dispatcher end-to-end -- not a static code read. customer_journey_complete stays false and operational_status stays 'PILOT ONLY' (not 'GA APPROVED') pending a real dynamic_browser click-through pass, per this registry's own Production Truth Law (docs/ENGINEERING_STANDARDS.md §11) -- no live browser session was available in this environment. Deliberately out of scope, disclosed not silently skipped: file attachments (no upload infrastructure exists anywhere in the repo to build on -- confirmed via repo-wide search; would need new R2 wiring, out of scope per this task's own 'if backend supports them' framing), and an admin/support-staff ticket-triage UI (the existing admin-only GET /api/support/tickets list API is untouched; a dedicated queue UI is a separate future concern, likely in the existing staff-facing admin portal page).

notes: Supersedes the 2026-07-14 finding above ('backend exists, frontend missing') -- both sides now exist and are wired together. The pre-existing CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods gap (workers/src/middleware/cors.js:49 omits PATCH) was found while building this feature but is a separate, cross-cutting, pre-existing issue affecting other already-shipped PATCH routes (e.g. workers/src/index.js:5570, :8309, :8342) -- recorded here per this repo's scope-discipline rule rather than folded into this PR; this feature's own status-update route was designed to use POST instead specifically to avoid depending on that bug. SUPPORT_PLAYBOOK.md's email/WhatsApp workflow remains the escalation path for logged-out visitors, who still see the mailto: links (frontend/user-dashboard.html:2720, :4161, unchanged).

name: First-Login Onboarding Checklist
description: ADDED 2026-07-15 (Customer Lifecycle Completion Program, Phase 6): a per-user setup checklist (organization profile, API key, first scan, asset inventory, alert preferences) showing real completion state, now reachable from the real post-login dashboard. Backend (handleOnboardingWizard, workers/src/handlers/commercialPlatformHandler.js, P15.1) already existed and was correctly computing 4 of 5 signals; its only UI was frontend/customer-success-dashboard.html, a page not linked from user-dashboard.html (the confirmed real post-login landing page, CAP-IDN-001) or any other reachable nav -- customer-facing, this feature was invisible. Ported the checklist UI into user-dashboard.html as a new 'Getting Started' page (real session auth via the page's existing apiFetch(), not the old page's weaker paste-your-API-key gate).

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html: nav item ("🚀 Getting Started", placed directly under "Dashboard" in the Overview section, the most prominent slot in the sidebar), #page-onboarding (progress bar + step list), loadOnboarding() calling the real GET /api/customer/onboarding/wizard via the page's existing apiFetch() (same session-token auth every other page on this dashboard uses). Browser-verified via a real headless-Chromium Playwright session against the served, modified file (python3 -m http.server locally, not live production) with contract-accurate mocked API responses (response shape copied directly from handleOnboardingWizard's source, not guessed): completion %, progress bar width, status badge, and all 5 step titles/descriptions render correctly for both a partial (60%, 3/5) and complete (100%, 5/5, status COMPLETE, next_step null) response; zero new console/page errors (the only 2 errors captured were pre-existing Google Fonts CDN failures from this sandbox's lack of external network access, unrelated to this change); existing Dashboard/Overview nav confirmed unaffected. NOT YET verified against the live deployed backend/D1 with a real account -- this was a local-mock dynamic_browser pass, not a live-production one.

navigation.evidence: Real nav-item in the Overview section of the sidebar, second position (directly below the Dashboard/Overview link itself) -- the most prominent placement available, chosen specifically because this feature's whole purpose is guiding new customers.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/commercialPlatform.test.mjs: 2 new tests (of 78 total in the file, up from 76) proving (a) the 'notifications' step (title 'Alert Preferences') now reflects a real notification_preferences row instead of a hardcoded false, and (b) completion_pct can now reach 100% when all 5 real signals are true -- both scenarios were previously impossible to test meaningfully because the 5th step could never complete. A latent pre-existing gap in this test file's own DB mock was found and fixed in the same pass: the mock's assetExists option was only wired into the .all() query path, but handleOnboardingWizard's real assets check calls .first() -- so assetExists:true was silently a no-op for this handler's own tests before now (harmless: no prior test asserted on it), fixed by adding the missing .first() branch.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/commercialPlatformHandler.js read directly in full. Found and fixed a second, real defect beyond the discoverability gap this entry primarily addresses: the 'notifications' step was hardcoded completed:false with no query at all -- meaning completion_pct could never reach 100% regardless of what a real customer did. Fixed to check notification_preferences (the same table CAP-NOTIF-001's real Settings 'Team Alert Channels' card writes to). Both fixes (frontend port + backend completion-check fix) verified together: full worker suite (308 files/3217 tests) green, and a real headless-Chromium Playwright session against the served, modified frontend/user-dashboard.html (locally served, contract-accurate mocked API responses, not live production) confirmed the checklist renders correctly end-to-end, including the previously-broken 'Alert Preferences' step, for both partial and 100%-complete states. customer_journey_complete stays false pending a post-merge production smoke test against the live deployed backend/D1 with a real account, per this registry's own Production Truth Law (docs/ENGINEERING_STANDARDS.md §11) -- consistent with how CAP-NOTIF-002 treated the same local-mock-vs-live-production distinction.

notes: Scope note: frontend/customer-success-dashboard.html is a much larger page than this single feature -- it also contains License Center (P15.2), Usage Analytics (P15.3), Success Score (P15.4), API Key Management V2 (P15.6), Report Archive (P15.7), Notification Center (P15.8), and Commercial Observability (P15.9), none of which were audited or ported in this pass. Several likely duplicate already-working equivalents elsewhere in user-dashboard.html (API keys, subscriptions/billing, reports/downloads are all separately confirmed CAP-PORTAL-001/002 'exists'), and sales-crm.json's CAP entry for a 'customer success score' documents a THIRD, separate implementation of that specific concept -- a real, pre-existing duplication this pass did not attempt to reconcile. frontend/customer-success-dashboard.html itself was deliberately left untouched (not deleted, not redirected) rather than guessed at, since 7 of its 9 sections were never individually verified as live-vs-duplicate-vs-dead. Recommended as a dedicated future audit, not a quick follow-on. Notification Center (P15.8) specifically was evaluated and NOT ported: its backend (handleNotificationCenter) reads ops_notifications, a table nothing in the codebase ever writes to (Finding 4, docs/audit-history/PHASE6_CUSTOMER_ENGAGEMENT_AUDIT_2026-07-15.md -- not yet its own registry entry, flagged as an open architecture decision) -- porting it now would only relocate a permanently-empty page, and it would sit confusingly next to the already-real, already-populated 'Team Alert Channels' delivery log already in Settings (CAP-NOTIF-001).
